source_docs.title: SHARE-seq Supplementary Figure 1 and workflow companion

oligos.name: Read1 (Tn5 mosaic-end adapter A)
oligos.role: Tn5 transposon adapter carrying the Nextera Read 1 sequencing primer + mosaic end; loaded onto Tn5 for ATAC (and for cDNA tagmentation of the RNA library)
oligos.notes: HPLC, 100 nM. Annealed to Blocked_ME_Comp before Tn5 assembly. 3' AGATGTGTATAAGAGACAG = mosaic end (ME).

oligos.name: Phosphorylated Read2 (Tn5 mosaic-end adapter B)
oligos.role: Second Tn5 transposon adapter; its 5'-phosphate is used for downstream barcode ligation; provides the Read 2 / ligation handle on ATAC fragments
oligos.notes: 5'-phosphorylated (/5Phos/), HPLC, 100 nM. Annealed to Blocked_ME_Comp. 5' GTCTCGTGGGCTCGG is the universal ligation/Read2 handle shared with the RT primer.

oligos.name: Blocked ME-complement
oligos.role: Mosaic-end complement strand of the Tn5 transposon; blocks self-tagmentation and polymerase extension
oligos.notes: /5Phos/ + phosphorothioate backbone (C*T*G*T*C*T*C*T*T*A*T*A*C*A) + /3ddC/. 5'Phos enables tagmentation; 3'ddC prevents polymerase extension; phosphorothioates block self-tagmentation.

oligos.name: RT primer (biotinylated poly(dT)+UMI)
oligos.role: Reverse-transcription primer priming on mRNA poly(A); adds ligation handle, UMI and biotin to first-strand cDNA

components.name: ligation/Read2 handle + ME
components.role: 5'-phosphorylated universal overhang for barcode ligation (same handle as phospho-Read2)

components.name: biotin-dT
components.role: internal biotinylated dT (/iBiodT/) for streptavidin pulldown

components.name: anchor
components.role: 3' anchored base (V=A/C/G, N=A/C/G/T) — degenerate, not ACGTN-encodable

oligos.notes: /5Phos/...NNNNNNNNNN/iBiodT/TTTTTTTTTTTTTTVN. HPLC, 100 nM. 5'-phosphate used for subsequent barcode ligation.

oligos.name: Template-switch oligo (TSO)
oligos.role: Template-switch oligo captured at the 3' end of first-strand cDNA during Maxima H- template switching
oligos.notes: Original AAGCAGTGGTATCAACGCAGAGTGAATrGrG+G (riboG-riboG-LNA G) folded to ...GAATGGG. HPLC, 100 nM.

oligos.name: RNA PCR primer
oligos.role: cDNA amplification primer binding the TSO handle after template switching
oligos.notes: STD, 100 nM. Used with P7 primer to amplify template-switched cDNA.

oligos.name: P7 primer
oligos.role: Universal P7 PCR primer used for both ATAC and RNA library amplification
oligos.notes: STD, 100 nM. Also the 5' portion of every Round 3 barcode oligo.

oligos.name: Round 1 linker
oligos.role: Splint linker strand annealed to Round 1 barcode strands to create the ligatable barcode cassette
oligos.notes: STD, 1 µM. Universal linker partially complementary to well-specific Round 1 barcodes.

oligos.name: Round 2 linker
oligos.role: Splint linker strand for Round 2 barcode ligation

oligos.name: Round 3 linker
oligos.role: Splint linker strand for Round 3 barcode ligation

oligos.name: Round 1 blocking strand
oligos.role: Blocks the free 5' overhang of unligated Round 1 barcodes to prevent mislabeling in later rounds
oligos.notes: STD, 1 µM; added at 22 µM after round 1.

oligos.name: Round 2 blocking strand
oligos.notes: STD, 1 µM; added at 26.4 µM after round 2.

oligos.name: Round 3 blocking strand
oligos.notes: STD, 1 µM; added at 23 µM after round 3.

oligos.name: Round 1 barcode strand (96-plex)
oligos.role: First cell-barcode round; 5'-phosphorylated strand ligated onto the cDNA/chromatin ligation handle
oligos.notes: /5Phos/. Example (Round1_01, barcode ACGTGATC): CGCGCTGCATACTTGAACGTGATCCCATGATCGTCCGA. 96 barcodes per plate.

oligos.name: Round 2 barcode strand (96-plex)
oligos.role: Second cell-barcode round; ligated 5' of Round 1

oligos.name: Round 3 barcode strand / P7 index (96-plex)
oligos.role: Third cell-barcode round; terminal strand carrying the P7 sequence for library capture/amplification

oligos.name: Custom Ad1 adapter (i5 index), plate Ad1 (96-plex)
oligos.role: Library-specific P5 + i5 index + Read1 primer added by PCR to both ATAC and tagmented-cDNA libraries

components.name: Read1 handle
components.role: constant, partial Nextera Read 1 (ends before AAGAGACAG)

final_library.source_label: SHARE-seq RNA (cDNA) library — top strand 5'→3' (ATAC library shares the same barcode/P5/P7 architecture without the UMI/poly-dT)
annotation_lines: TCGTCGGCAGCGTCAGATGTGTATAAGAGACAG   = Nextera Read 1 primer + mosaic end (Read 1 start)
[CDNA]                               = cDNA insert (RNA library) / genomic insert (ATAC library)
[UMI:10]                             = 10 nt UMI (RNA only; read at start of Read 2 as reverse complement)
CCGAGCCCACGAGACTCGGACGATCATGGG       = Read 2 / ligation handle (rev-comp of GTCTCGTGGGCTCGG...) + Round1 junction
[CELL_BARCODE:8] x3                  = three split-pool ligation barcodes B1, B2, B3 (read in Index 1, 99 cyc)
ATCTCGTATGCCGTCTTCTGCTTG             = P7 (reverse complement on top strand)

evidence.locator: SHARE-seq RNA (cDNA) library — top strand 5'→3' (ATAC library shares the same barcode/P5/P7 architecture without the UMI/poly-dT)

reads.primer: i7 index read (from P7 end into the barcode cassette)

library_generation.title: Assemble Tn5 transposomes
library_generation.summary: Anneal Read1 and phosphorylated-Read2 adapters to Blocked ME-complement and load onto in-house Tn5.
library_generation.note: Two transposomes are formed sharing the mosaic end: one carrying the Nextera Read1 handle, one carrying the 5'-phosphorylated Read2/ligation handle (GTCTCGTGGGCTCGG...). ME-comp is 3'-ddC/phosphorothioate to prevent self-tagmentation.
library_generation.product:
Read1 adapter:          5'- TCGTCGGCAGCGTCAGATGTGTATAAGAGACAG -3'
                                   3'-        CACATATTCTCTGTC -5' (ddC)

Phospho-Read2 adapter: 5'p- GTCTCGTGGGCTCGGAGATGTGTATAAGAGACAG -3'
                                    3'-        CACATATTCTCTGTC -5' (ddC)

library_generation.title: Transposition (ATAC)
library_generation.summary: Tagment fixed, permeabilized cells/nuclei so Tn5 inserts Read1 and Read2 handles into open chromatin.
library_generation.note: Marks accessible genomic DNA; leaves 9 bp gaps filled later. Chromatin fragments now carry the universal Read2/ligation handle for barcoding.
library_generation.product:
5'- TCGTCGGCAGCGTCAGATGTGTATAAGAGACAG [genomic DNA] CTGTCTCTTATACAC...      -3'
         ...       p- GTCTCGTGGGCTCGGAGATGTGTATAAGAGACAG [genomic DNA] ...   -3'

library_generation.title: Reverse transcription (RNA)
library_generation.summary: Reverse-transcribe mRNA with the biotinylated poly(dT)+UMI RT primer, adding the ligation handle and UMI to cDNA.
library_generation.note: The RT primer's 5' end carries the same GTCTCGTGGGCTCGG ligation handle plus a 10 nt UMI and internal biotin, so cDNA and chromatin become barcode-compatible in the same tube.
library_generation.product:
mRNA:  5'- [.....transcript.....] AAAAAAAAAAAA -3'
cDNA:           3'-...[cDNA]...VN TTTTTTTTTTTTTT(bio)[UMI] GACAGAAATATGTGTAGAGGCTCGGGTGCTCTG -p5'
                                                  (handle: GTCTCGTGGGCTCGGAGATGTGTATAAGAGACAG)

library_generation.title: Round 1 split-pool barcoding
library_generation.summary: Distribute cells into a 96-well plate, hybridize Round 1 barcodes, then add Round 1 blocking strands.
library_generation.note: Well-specific Round 1 barcode (annealed to Round 1 linker) hybridizes to the ligation handle on both cDNA and chromatin; blocking strands cap unligated overhangs before pooling.

library_generation.title: Round 2 split-pool barcoding
library_generation.summary: Pool, re-split into a second 96-well plate, hybridize Round 2 barcodes, then block.
library_generation.note: Round 2 barcode ligates 5' of Round 1 via its constant junction (ATCCACGTGCTTGAG / CGCGCTGCATACTTGA).

library_generation.title: Round 3 barcoding + simultaneous ligation
library_generation.summary: Re-split into a third plate, hybridize Round 3 (P7-bearing) barcodes and block, then T4-ligate all barcodes onto cDNA and chromatin at once.
library_generation.note: Round 3 barcode carries the terminal P7. After hybridization, T4 DNA ligase seals the full B1-B2-B3 cassette onto both molecule types, yielding ~96^3 = 884,736 cell barcodes.

library_generation.title: Reverse crosslinking & release
library_generation.summary: Proteinase K reverse-crosslinking releases barcoded cDNA and chromatin fragments into solution.

library_generation.title: Streptavidin separation
library_generation.summary: Capture biotinylated cDNA on MyOne C1 streptavidin beads; supernatant retains transposed chromatin (ATAC).
library_generation.note: This physically splits the shared pool into an RNA (bead-bound) and an ATAC (supernatant) fraction that carry identical cell barcodes.
library_generation.product: Beads: biotin-[cDNA + barcodes]      Supernatant: [chromatin fragments + barcodes]

library_generation.title: ATAC library PCR
library_generation.summary: Amplify the supernatant with library-specific Ad1 (P5-i5-Read1) primer + P7 primer to complete the ATAC library.
library_generation.note: Adds P5 + i5 index and Read1 primer on the transposed end; P7 is already present from Round 3.

library_generation.title: cDNA amplification, tagmentation & RNA library PCR
library_generation.summary: Template-switch and amplify bead cDNA (RNA PCR primer + P7), tagment with Read1-Tn5, then PCR with Ad1 (P5-i5-Read1) + P7 to finish the RNA library.
library_generation.note: TSO adds the RNA_PCR_primer handle; PCR amplifies full-length cDNA; a second Read1-only Tn5 fragments the cDNA and adds P5/i5/Read1 at internal positions, keeping the barcode/UMI/P7 end intact.

library_sequencing.note: Nextera Read 1 primer + mosaic end; reads into the cDNA insert (RNA library) or one genomic end (ATAC library).
Reads the shared cell identity: three split-pool ligation barcodes B3-B2-B1 (8 bp each) separated by their constant linkers. 99 cycles span B3 + linker + B2 + linker + B1.
library_sequencing.diagram:
i7 index primer ------------------------------------------------> (99 cyc)
 5'-CAAGCAGAAGACGGCATACGAGAT
5'- CAAGCAGAAGACGGCATACGAGAT [B3] GTGGCCGATGTTTCGCATCGGCGTACGACTA [B2] ATCCACGTGCTTGAGCGCGCTGCATACTTGA [B1] CCCATGATCGTCCGA... -3'
3'- GTTCGTCTTCTGCCGTATGCTCTA [B3] CACCGGCTACAAAGCGTAGCCGCATGCTGAT [B2] TAGGTGCACGAACTCGCGCGACGTATGAACT [B1] GGGTACTAGCAGGCT... -5'

library_sequencing.note: Reads the 8 bp i5 sample/library index (plate Ad1) that distinguishes PCR sub-libraries.
library_sequencing.diagram:
i5 index primer  ----------> (8 cyc)
 5'-AATGATACGGCGACCACCGAGATCTACAC
5'- AATGATACGGCGACCACCGAGATCTACAC [i5] TCGTCGGCAGCGTCAGATGTGTAT... -3'
3'- TTACTATGCCGCTGGTGGCTCTAGATGTG [i5] AGCAGCCGTCGCAGTCTACACATA... -5'

library_sequencing.note: RNA library: reads the 10 bp UMI, then the poly-dT (bases 11-16 must be TTTTTT), then into the cDNA. ATAC library: reads the second genomic end.

title: SHARE-seq
description: SHARE-seq (Simultaneous High-throughput ATAC and RNA Expression with sequencing) jointly measures chromatin accessibility and gene expression in the same single cell. Fixed cells/nuclei are tagmented by Tn5 to mark open chromatin, mRNA is reverse-transcribed with a biotinylated poly(dT)+UMI primer, and both molecule types receive the same cell identity through three rounds of split-pool hybridization/ligation barcoding (~96^3 combinations). Streptavidin pulldown separates cDNA from chromatin, and two libraries (RNA and ATAC) are built and sequenced. Reads capture the shared three-part cell barcode (index read), the cDNA or genomic insert (Read 1 / Read 2), and a 10 bp RNA UMI (Read 2).

reference.label: SHARE-seq Supplementary Figure 1 and workflow companion

publication.statistical_model: Spearman correlation with chromVAR GC/accessibility-matched background and one-sided z-test for peak-gene cis-associations; Poisson (Lander-Waterman) model for library-size estimation; birthday-paradox model with Z-tests for barcode collision rate.

original_publication.title: Chromatin Potential Identified by Shared Single-Cell Profiling of RNA and Chromatin

throughput.summary: Massively scalable split-pool co-assay: ~2 million cells per run at ~1% collision; ~$433 library-prep cost per 100,000 cells. 84,426 cells profiled across 4 cell lines and 3 tissues; 34,774 joint mouse-skin profiles.
throughput.cells: 84,426 total; human/mouse mix gave 903 human + 1,341 mouse cells at 0.04% collision; ~2M cells/run capacity
throughput.rna: avg 2,545 RNA UMIs per cell (9,660 estimated UMI library size)
throughput.dna: avg 8,252 unique ATAC fragments per cell (19,723 estimated library size; 65.5% fragments in peaks)

other.label: Concept
other.value: Defines DORCs and 'chromatin potential' — chromatin accessibility foreshadows gene expression during lineage commitment

data_processing.summary: Two parallel pipelines share the same three-round split-pool cell barcode: an ATAC branch (trim, demultiplex barcodes, bowtie2 alignment, MACS2 peak calling, chromVAR fragment/TF scoring) and an RNA branch (trim + poly-dT filter, STAR alignment, featureCounts exon/intron annotation, UMI-Tools UMI collapse, Seurat/UMAP). Paired single-cell profiles are matched by the common well-barcode combination.
data_processing.statistical_model: One-sided z-test against a chromVAR GC/accessibility-matched background for peak–gene cis-associations; Poisson (Lander–Waterman) model for library-size estimation.
